departments.concept: Organizational units of the clinic. Use this for macro-level analysis of hospital operations, patient flow by wing, and departmental workload distribution.

columns_with_descriptions.name: Clinical division name. USE THIS for categorical business grouping.
columns_with_descriptions.location: Geographic location of the department within the hospital facility.
columns_with_descriptions.operating_budget: Annual financial allocation [FLOAT]. Use this for ROI and cost-efficiency analysis.

staff.concept: Hospital human resources. Use this to analyze practitioner performance, track doctor-patient ratios, and resource allocation across departments.

columns_with_descriptions.first_name: Given name of the clinician or administrator.
columns_with_descriptions.role: Professional designation. Use this to filter clinical vs. administrative queries.
columns_with_descriptions.specialisation: Specific medical focus (e.g., 'Cardiologist', 'Pediatrician'). Use this for precise expertise queries.
columns_with_descriptions.department_id: [FK -> departments.id] Establishes the primary work-unit of the staff member.

patients.concept: Central clinical registry. This is the root table for all patient-centric metrics, demographics, and longitudinal health tracking.

columns_with_descriptions.id: [PK] Unique clinical identifier for the patient.
columns_with_descriptions.date_of_birth: Legal birth date [DATE]. Crucial for age-based clinical bucketing and analysis.
columns_with_descriptions.nhi_number: National Health Index value; the unique medical record key.
columns_with_descriptions.gender: Biological demographic. Used for gender-specific health screening analysis.
columns_with_descriptions.risk_profile: Calculated clinical risk score (0-100) [FLOAT]. High values indicate urgent screening needs.

appointments.concept: Clinical encounters and utilization events. Links patients to doctors and departments.

columns_with_descriptions.id: [PK] Unique record of the clinical encounter.
columns_with_descriptions.patient_id: [FK -> patients.id] Links the visit to a specific individual.
columns_with_descriptions.department_id: [FK -> departments.id] Tracks which clinical unit hosted the visit.
columns_with_descriptions.appointment_date: Timestamp of the clinical interaction. Used for longitudinal trend analysis.
columns_with_descriptions.status: Outcome of the scheduled visit. Use 'Completed' for reality-based metrics.
columns_with_descriptions.reason: Narrative reason for the visit stated by the patient.

billing.concept: Financial health and revenue cycle management. Use this to calculate hospital income and outstanding liabilities.

columns_with_descriptions.appointment_id: [FK -> appointments.id] Links financial charges to a clinical visit.
columns_with_descriptions.amount: The monetary value of the service [NUMERIC]. Sum this for revenue analysis.
columns_with_descriptions.invoice_date: Tax/Accounting date for the billing event.

medical_supplies.concept: Inventory and logistical management. Use this for supply-chain analysis and overhead tracking.

columns_with_descriptions.reorder_level: Threshold at which stock must be replenished.
columns_with_descriptions.department_id: [FK -> departments.id] Assigns ownership and responsibility for supply stock.

lab_results.concept: Pathology and diagnostic data. Use this to identify clinical risks and trends.

columns_with_descriptions.appointment_id: [FK -> appointments.id] Contextualizes the test within a visit.

prescriptions.concept: Pharmacotherapy management. Use this to track prescribed drugs and dosages.

columns_with_descriptions.appointment_id: [FK -> appointments.id] Links medication to a visit.

clinical_notes.concept: Rich narrative clinical documentation. Use this to identify specific symptoms, lifestyle notes, or triage patterns via SQL joins.

columns_with_descriptions.appointment_id: [FK -> appointments.id] Links the note to a visit.
columns_with_descriptions.content: The actual narrative medical note text.
